cards.title: Family Estrangement: Research Shows Mixed Wellbeing Effects
cards.body: Estrangement exists on a continuum rather than as a binary choice. Support plays a crucial role in helping people navigate reduced contact with family members.

cards.title: Parent Guilt: Learning Lessons From Past Mistakes
cards.body: Parents often dwell on past mistakes, but these reveal lessons needed in the present. Understanding this shift can help reduce guilt and improve parenting going forward.

cards.title: Child Mental Healthcare and Systemic Racism: What Parents Should Know
cards.body: Child psychiatry has racist origins that continue to affect care for children of color today. Parents of color should understand this history before seeking mental healthcare.

cards.title: Emotional Intelligence in Children: Naming Feelings Matters
cards.body: Teaching children to name their emotions gives them agency over their feelings. This simple practice is the number one sign of emotional intelligence development.

cards.title: Families After Dementia Diagnosis: Planning Care and Connection
cards.body: A dementia diagnosis affects more than just memory—it reshapes family dynamics. Families can plan for care, protect connection, and support caregivers before crisis hits.

cards.title: When Unhealthy Relationships Feel Familiar to Children
cards.body: Abuse survivors often blame themselves for missing warning signs. But when harmful behavior was normalized in childhood, it feels disturbingly familiar and safe.

cards.title: When a Parent Suffers Moral Injury in System Failures
cards.body: Parents 'losing control' often experience accumulated frustration in systems designed to overrule them. Understanding moral injury helps parents recognize their valid emotional responses.

cards.title: Affordable Childcare: Government Spending Billions to Help Families
cards.body: The government is investing billions to expand affordable childcare access for families. Stopping profiteers who undermine these benefits is a key priority.

cards.title: Supporting Communication Differences: AAC and Nonspeaking Children
cards.body: There are multiple reasons why children may use augmentative and alternative communication (AAC). Learning to support communication differences helps all children thrive.
